intro_title: A multidisciplinary profile in science and education
intro_text: Vadym Shved is an interdisciplinary chemist, chemistry educator, and teacher in training with academic experience across Ukraine, France, and Switzerland. His background combines chemistry, nanotechnology, applied physics, laboratory science, and pedagogy, together with years of tutoring and teaching experience in multilingual and international learning contexts.

about_summary_html: I am an interdisciplinary chemist, chemistry educator, and teacher in training with an academic background spanning Ukraine, France, and Switzerland. My educational path includes a Bachelor’s degree in Chemistry, a Master’s degree in Applied Physics and Nanomaterials, and a second Master’s degree in Nanoscale Engineering from École Centrale de Lyon. I am currently enrolled at PHBern in the teaching diploma programme for upper secondary schools in chemistry, further strengthening my profile at the intersection of science, pedagogy, and international education.
about_bio_studies_html: This early passion led me to study Chemistry at Taras Shevchenko National University of Kyiv, where I built a strong scientific foundation and developed a growing interest in interdisciplinary science. Later, I continued with a Master’s degree in Applied Physics and Nanomaterials, and then completed a second Master’s degree in Nanoscale Engineering in France at École Centrale de Lyon. These studies gave me an academic path that connects chemistry with physics, nanotechnology, biology, materials science, and modern laboratory research. Today, I continue this journey in Switzerland, where I am training as a chemistry teacher for upper secondary schools at PHBern.

about_worked_as_a_chemistry_teacher_in: Worked as a chemistry teacher in a private engineering school in Kyiv. Taught chemistry in an applied and student-oriented way, helping learners develop stronger scientific understanding through structured explanation and practical examples.
about_provided_long_term_private_tutorin: Provided long-term private tutoring in chemistry for school students and other learners. This work included regular subject support, concept explanation, and exam-oriented preparation, and became the foundation of my broader teaching practice.
about_offering_online_chemistry_tutoring: Offering online chemistry tutoring for learners in Switzerland, including support for school-level chemistry, upper-secondary chemistry, and individualized academic guidance.

about_worked_as_a_substitute_chemistry_t: Worked as a substitute chemistry teacher in high school classes, teaching in a Swiss school environment and supporting continuity in subject instruction, laboratory experiments, and a project week.
about_worked_as_a_substitute_teacher_in: Worked as a substitute teacher in a secondary school setting (Nature & Technology, Mathematics, Individual Preparation, Physical Education), gaining broader practical teaching experience in the Swiss education system.

about_worked_as_a_substitute_teacher_in_: Worked as a substitute teacher in a Swiss secondary school (Nature & Technology), gaining broader classroom experience across different learner groups.
Worked as a substitute teacher in English and German, broadening my teaching profile beyond chemistry and strengthening my multilingual educational practice.
about_taught_chemistry_as_a_substitute_t: Taught chemistry as a substitute teacher in FMS classes, including the Fachmaturität Pädagogik track.
about_worked_in_an_interdisciplinary_pro: Worked in an interdisciplinary project environment connected to Horizon 2020 MILEAGE. This role combined project support, scientific communication, and interdisciplinary work in an international innovation environment.
about_completed_a_master_s_thesis_focuse: Completed a Master’s thesis focused on the functionalization of ultrafine nanoparticles for active targeting of receptors of interest in oncology. This work strengthened my research profile in nanochemistry, biomedical applications, and interdisciplinary laboratory science.
about_worked_in_supramolecular_chemistry: Worked in supramolecular chemistry while developing my Bachelor’s thesis, gaining early research experience in molecular interactions, synthesis, and practical laboratory work.

about_interdisciplinary_work_connecting_: Interdisciplinary work connecting chemistry, materials, and physics.
about_my_work_is_shaped_by_clarity: My work is shaped by clarity, curiosity, precision, openness, and long-term growth. I care about scientific depth, but I also care about how people understand, feel, and develop while learning. This gives my work a distinctly multilingual and intercultural dimension.
about_a_fuller_biographical_narrative_co: A fuller biographical narrative connecting early scientific interests, university studies, interdisciplinary research, and the long-term development of my teaching profile.
about_from_an_early_age_i_was: From an early age, I was drawn to science and especially to chemistry. During my school years, that interest grew through Olympiads, advanced study, and a lasting curiosity about how the natural world works. My school path was already deeply connected with science: I studied in a chemistry- and biology-focused environment, took part in regional and national chemistry competitions, and gradually discovered that chemistry was not only a subject I enjoyed, but a field that truly shaped the way I think.
about_alongside_my_studies_i_have_been: Alongside my studies, I have been teaching chemistry since the age of eighteen. What started with helping classmates and younger students gradually became a long-term commitment to tutoring, mentoring, and teaching learners from different countries and educational systems. Over the years, I have worked with school students, high school students, and university beginners, including preparation for demanding programs such as IB Diploma Chemistry. My teaching approach is shaped by both science and empathy: I believe chemistry should be taught clearly, patiently, and with real understanding, not just memorization.
about_a_structured_overview_of_my_scient: A structured overview of my scientific, pedagogical, analytical, and interdisciplinary competencies developed through academic training, laboratory work, and teaching experience.
about_my_professional_background_brings_: My professional background brings together two closely connected paths: teaching and education, and chemistry-based scientific work. Over the years, I have developed both as an educator and as an interdisciplinary chemist, combining classroom teaching, private tutoring, laboratory work, and research-oriented project experience in Ukraine, France, and Switzerland.

bachelor_research_institution_taras_shev: InstitutionTaras Shevchenko National University of Kyiv
bachelor_research_institute_department_i: Institute / DepartmentInstitute of High Technologies
bachelor_research_the_project_can_later_: The project can later describe why calixarene-derived model molecules are relevant for supramolecular chemistry, how they relate to intermolecular interaction analysis, and why this topic was meaningful in the context of early-stage nanochemistry-oriented research training.
bachelor_research_the_objectives_block_i: The objectives block is reserved for a concise explanation of the synthesis goals, the intended functionalization strategy, and the broader conceptual purpose of using these compounds as model molecules.
bachelor_research_the_methods_block_is_r: The methods block is reserved for a clean description of the synthetic pathway, ester formation, and the chemistry-oriented analytical logic used during the project.
bachelor_research_the_results_section_is: The results section is reserved for a concise discussion of the successful synthesis, the scientific direction of the project, and the broader value of the molecules for interaction-focused supramolecular investigation.
bachelor_research_this_page_is_reserved_: This page is reserved for a structured summary of the bachelor research project on the functionalization of hexacalixresorcinarene derivatives and the synthesis of their esters for the study of intermolecular interactions in supramolecular and nanochemical contexts.
bachelor_research_this_section_is_reserv: This section is reserved for molecular schemes, reaction diagrams, selected visuals, synthetic pathways, or laboratory-oriented figures that support the project without publishing the full thesis.
bachelor_research_this_project_can_later: This project can later be linked to the master project, mini-projects in nanochemistry, or additional chemistry presentations completed during the academic program.
master_research_the_background_section_c: The background section can later explain the immune-therapy context, the biological rationale for receptor blocking, and the broader scientific motivation for nanoparticle-assisted targeted therapeutic strategies.
master_research_the_objectives_block_is_: The objectives block is reserved for the project aims: functionalization of nanoparticles, attachment of biologically active targeting elements, and development of a relevant bioconjugation concept for future therapeutic application.
master_research_the_methods_section_is_r: The methods section is reserved for a concise description of click chemistry, nanoparticle functionalization, and the logic of bioconjugate formation in an interdisciplinary nanochemistry and biomedical research setting.
master_research_the_results_and_directio: The results and direction section is reserved for a summary of the project logic, scientific direction, and the relevance of the bioconjugation concept for selective targeting and potential cancer-therapy applications.
master_research_this_page_is_reserved_fo: This page is reserved for a structured summary of the master research project focused on the bioconjugation of AGuIX nanoparticles with antibody-based targeting elements using click chemistry in a cancer-therapy-oriented biomedical context.
master_research_this_section_is_reserved: This section is reserved for nanoparticle diagrams, click-chemistry workflows, conceptual biomedical figures, or project-specific visual explanations prepared for portfolio presentation.
master_research_this_page_can_later_be_l: This page can later be linked to the bachelor thesis, related nanochemistry presentations, and future mini-project pages in biomedical and chemistry-oriented topics.
